title: Typeform tokens should not be disclosed
description: In most cases, trust boundaries are violated when a secret is exposed in a source code repository or an uncontrolled deployment environment. Unintended people who don’t need to know the secret might get access to it. They might then be able to use it to gain unwanted access to associated services or resources.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: Typeform Personal Access Token detected. Typeform Personal Access Tokens (starting with 'tfp_') provide programmatic access to the Typeform API, enabling full control over forms, responses, workspaces, and account settings. These tokens can create, modify, and delete forms, access all form responses including sensitive personal data, manage workspace members and permissions, retrieve detailed analytics and reporting data, configure webhooks and integrations, access billing information and subscription details, modify account settings and branding configurations, and export form data in various formats. When a Typeform Personal Access Token is compromised, attackers can steal all form responses containing personally identifiable information (PII), customer feedback, survey data, and business intelligence, create malicious forms that impersonate your brand for phishing attacks, delete existing forms and destroy valuable data collection workflows, modify form questions to collect additional sensitive information without consent, access user management functions to add or remove team members, configure unauthorized webhooks to intercept form submissions in real-time, access billing information and potentially modify subscription settings, export large datasets for competitive intelligence or data resale, manipulate form analytics to hide or distort business metrics, and use your Typeform account to send spam or conduct social engineering attacks. The impact can be severe including regulatory compliance violations under GDPR, CCPA, and other privacy laws due to unauthorized access to personal data, significant financial losses from premium feature abuse and potential legal liabilities, brand reputation damage from malicious forms created under your account, loss of competitive advantage through stolen survey and market research data, operational disruption from deleted or modified critical forms, potential customer data breaches affecting trust and business relationships, and exposure of confidential business information through analytics and response data. Immediately secure your Typeform integration by logging into your Typeform account, navigating to Account Settings > Personal tokens, revoking the compromised token immediately, generating a new Personal Access Token with minimal required scopes, updating all applications, scripts, CI/CD pipelines, and integrations with the new token, reviewing Typeform audit logs to identify unauthorized activities during the exposure period, checking all forms for unauthorized modifications or new malicious forms, verifying workspace member lists for any unauthorized additions, reviewing webhook configurations and disabling any suspicious endpoints, examining form analytics for unusual access patterns or data export activities, conducting a comprehensive security audit of all connected systems and workflows, implementing IP address restrictions and access controls where possible, using secure environment variables or dedicated secret management systems to store the new token, enabling two-factor authentication on your Typeform account, setting up monitoring and alerting for unusual API usage patterns, regularly rotating access tokens as part of security best practices, documenting this incident for compliance and audit purposes, and notifying affected customers if personal data may have been compromised.

Typeform API token detected. Typeform API tokens provide programmatic access to create, manage, and retrieve data from Typeform surveys, quizzes, and forms. These tokens can access form definitions, retrieve all submitted responses including sensitive personal information, create and modify forms programmatically, manage workspace settings and team members, configure webhooks and data integrations, access form analytics and completion statistics, export response data in various formats, and control form sharing and publication settings. When compromised, attackers can steal all form response data potentially containing PII, customer feedback, market research, employee information, and confidential business data, create fraudulent forms that appear to come from your organization for phishing or social engineering attacks, delete or modify existing forms to disrupt business operations, access user management functions to compromise team accounts, configure malicious webhooks to intercept form submissions, manipulate form analytics to hide suspicious activity, export datasets for competitive intelligence purposes, and abuse premium features leading to unexpected billing charges. This can result in serious regulatory compliance violations under data protection laws, significant reputational damage from brand impersonation, financial losses from service disruption and legal liabilities, loss of valuable market research and customer feedback data, privacy breaches affecting customer trust and business relationships, and exposure of confidential business intelligence and operational metrics. Take immediate action by accessing your Typeform account settings, revoking the exposed token and generating a replacement with minimal necessary permissions, updating all applications and integrations with the new credentials, reviewing audit logs for unauthorized access during the exposure window, checking all forms for unauthorized changes or additions, verifying team member access and removing any suspicious accounts, examining webhook configurations for malicious endpoints, analyzing form submission patterns for signs of data theft, implementing stronger access controls and IP restrictions where available, storing credentials securely in environment variables or secret management systems, enabling account security features like two-factor authentication, setting up monitoring for unusual API usage patterns, regularly rotating API tokens as a security best practice, and maintaining detailed incident documentation for compliance and security audit purposes.

Typeform API credential detected in assignment. This pattern identifies Typeform API tokens when assigned to variables or used in configuration settings. Typeform tokens provide comprehensive access to form management, response collection, workspace administration, and data analytics within the Typeform platform. When these credentials are exposed in source code, attackers can gain unauthorized control over your entire Typeform infrastructure to access all form responses containing potentially sensitive customer data, personal information, market research, and business intelligence, create malicious forms that impersonate your brand for phishing campaigns and social engineering attacks, delete or modify existing forms to disrupt business operations and data collection workflows, manipulate form settings to redirect submissions to attacker-controlled systems, access workspace management functions to add unauthorized users or modify team permissions, configure webhooks to intercept form data in real-time and exfiltrate information, export comprehensive datasets for competitive intelligence or unauthorized resale, access detailed analytics revealing business metrics and customer behavior patterns, abuse premium features leading to unexpected charges and service limitations, and leverage your account reputation to conduct fraudulent activities. The business impact can be devastating including massive data breaches exposing customer PII with resulting regulatory fines under GDPR, CCPA, and other privacy regulations, severe reputational damage from brand impersonation and customer data loss, operational disruption from deleted or compromised critical business forms, financial losses from premium feature abuse and potential legal liabilities, loss of competitive advantage through stolen market research and customer insights, compliance violations requiring extensive reporting and remediation efforts, customer trust erosion leading to business relationship damage, and exposure of confidential business strategies and operational data. Respond immediately by logging into your Typeform account dashboard, navigating to Account Settings and API tokens section, identifying and revoking the compromised token immediately, generating a new API token with strictly minimal required permissions, updating all applications, scripts, automation tools, and integration systems with the new credentials, conducting a thorough review of account audit logs to identify all unauthorized activities during the exposure timeframe, scanning all forms for unauthorized modifications, additions, or suspicious configuration changes, verifying workspace member lists and removing any unauthorized user accounts, examining webhook configurations and disabling any suspicious or unauthorized endpoints, analyzing form submission patterns and response data for signs of unauthorized access or data theft, reviewing billing statements for unexpected premium feature usage or charges, implementing stronger authentication controls including two-factor authentication, configuring IP address restrictions and access controls where supported, migrating credential storage to secure environment variables or dedicated secret management systems, establishing comprehensive monitoring and alerting for unusual API usage patterns, implementing regular token rotation schedules as a proactive security measure, conducting security training for team members on proper credential handling, documenting the incident thoroughly for compliance reporting and security audit purposes, and notifying affected customers and regulatory authorities if personal data was potentially compromised during the breach window.
